cot_prefix: Okay, let's try to figure out the answer to this question. The question is asking why weekend afternoons would become dead time without sports programs. Let me read through the passage again to make sure I understand the context. The passage says that sports are a significant source of income for TV companies. They use sports to attract specific audiences, which they then sell to advertisers. It mentions that sports events are popular on Saturday and Sunday afternoons, which are considered the slowest times for TV viewing. Without sports, those time slots would be "dead time." The key part here is the line: "Sport events are the most popular weekend programs, especially among male viewers who may not watch much television at other times during the week." So, during weekend afternoons, general TV viewing is low, but sports bring in viewers. If there were no sports, then those time slots would have low viewership, making them dead time. Looking at the answer choices: A) Because there would be few viewers B) Because the advertisers would be off work C) Because television programs would go slowly D) Because viewers would pay less for watching television The passage says that sports attract viewers during a time when TV viewing is normally slow. Without sports, the weekend afternoons would have low viewership. That matches with choice A. The passage also mentions that advertisers are willing to pay high fees because of the audience, but the question is about why the time would be dead without sports, not
